newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Only routine RNS filings: a Director/PDMR Shareholding notice on June 9 and a Total Voting Rights disclosure on June 4. The relevant fundamental datapoints all predate the window: the May AGM trading update (4-month organic revenue -4.8% on soft EMS demand, book-to-bill 107% led by aerospace & defence, FY26 guidance unchanged, GBP 3M net cost-programme benefit) and the January 2026 collapse of the Cicor takeover after shareholders rejected the scheme. Today's +1.56% move is small-cap noise, not fundamental.

sources.label: Investegate RNS feed for TT Electronics (TTG) - June 2026 filings are routine
Sharecast: TT Electronics reports mixed trading, in line with expectations (AGM update)
TipRanks: TT Electronics takeover by Cicor collapses as shareholders reject scheme

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved it up to reflect hidden passive/power-sensing content in AI-server power paths; nothing new since June 6 (only routine PDMR/voting-rights RNS) and a +1.56% day on a -0.5% YTD base leaves the residual-alpha setup unchanged, so hold rank 3.
verdicts.bull: Current-sense and thin/thick-film resistor content sits directly in AI power-delivery paths, the name is a $0.28B micro-cap nobody owns, and 107% book-to-bill plus failed-bid history (Volex, Cicor) gives both fundamental and M&A optionality at a flat YTD price.
verdicts.neutral: No fresh evidence since June 6 either way; AI-adjacent passives exposure is real but diluted by soft EMS and automation/electrification end-markets, so score drifts with the tape until the H1 results or a contract disclosure.
verdicts.bear: Group organic revenue is still shrinking (-4.8% in the 4 months to April) and the AI/passives content is a small slice of a mixed industrial portfolio, so the bottleneck thesis could remain unmonetized while turnaround execution risk dominates.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results / evidence of AI-server power and sensing design wins, or renewed bid interest post-Cicor collapse
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued EMS end-market softness and turnaround execution risk swamping the AI passives content

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 16: the latent edge-AI/power-components pathway is unchanged, no new contracts or capacity news since June 6, and yesterday's hold verdict stands absent genuinely new information.
verdicts.bull: Aerospace/defence demand (12% growth, structural tailwinds) and the China-to-Malaysia production shift strengthen exactly the regulated-market power and sensing franchise that the latent edge-AI thesis rests on.
verdicts.neutral: Speculative-conviction node with no June news flow; the thesis advances only on disclosed automation, defence-power or medical-electronics wins, none of which appeared this window.
verdicts.bear: A shrinking-revenue UK micro-cap with a failed takeover behind it may stay a generic EMS/components story; the edge-AI optionality is inferred, not evidenced.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Disclosed aerospace/defence power or automation/electrification design wins
verdicts.primaryRisk: Edge-AI angle never materializes beyond generic industrial electronics exposure

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday already marked it down to reconcile adjusted-score rank under the strict screen's medium-evidence bar; nothing since June 6 raises or lowers the evidence tier, so hold at 24 rather than compounding the move.
verdicts.bull: 107% book-to-bill and regulated-market sensors/power conversion give a credible migration path into rugged edge and thermal-control niches if AI-driven demand reaches defence and industrial platforms.
verdicts.neutral: Evidence remains medium under the strict lens: routine RNS only this window, and the rugged-edge pathway is plausible but undocumented by named programs.
verdicts.bear: Under a strict evidence standard the AI-chain link is the weakest of its three listings; soft EMS demand and a -37% adjusted EPS print last year argue the stock is a restructuring story, not an AI node.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Named rugged-edge or thermal-control program wins that harden the evidence tier
verdicts.primaryRisk: Strict-screen evidence never improves, leaving the name as low-grade industrial exposure on an AI list
